meanings.definition: social or financial or professional status or reputation
an ordered listing of scores or results showing the relative positions of competitors (individuals or teams) in a sporting event
the act of assuming or maintaining an erect upright position
be standing; be upright
examples: We had to stand for the entire performance!

meanings.definition: be in some specified state or condition
occupy a place or location, also metaphorically
hold one's ground; maintain a position; be steadfast or upright
examples: I am standing my ground and won't give in!

meanings.definition: put up with something or somebody unpleasant
examples: I cannot bear his constant criticism
The new secretary had to endure a lot of unprofessional remarks
he learned to tolerate the heat
She stuck out two years in a miserable marriage

meanings.definition: have or maintain a position or stand on an issue
examples: Where do you stand on the War?

meanings.definition: remain inactive or immobile
be in effect; be or remain in force
be tall; have a height of; copula
put into an upright position
examples: Can you stand the bookshelf up?

meanings.definition: withstand the force of something
examples: The mountain climbers had to fend against the ice and snow

meanings.definition: be available for stud services
examples: male domestic animals such as stallions serve selected females

meanings.definition: having a supporting base
not created for a particular occasion
(of fluids) not moving or flowing
executed in or initiated from a standing position
(of persons) on the feet; having the torso in an erect position supported by straight legs
permanent
